code2str.summary: Converts an ASCII code value into its corresponding character string and stores it in <strvar>. Multi-byte codes produce up to 4 characters; a zero input sets <strvar> to an empty string.

expandenv.summary: Expands environment variable references written as %name% to their values. With one argument it expands <strvar> in place; with two it expands <strval> and stores the result in <strvar>. Unknown variables are left unchanged.

int2str.summary: Converts <integer value> to its string representation and returns it in the string variable <strvar>. Equivalent to sprintf with the %d specifier.

regexoption.summary: Configures regex behavior for strmatch, strreplace, and waitregex: syntax style (Ruby/Perl/POSIX/Java), character encoding (UTF-8, ASCII, etc.), and options like case-insensitive or multiline. Defaults are Ruby syntax and UTF-8.

sprintf.summary: Produces C printf-style formatted output and stores the result in the system variable inputstr. Sets result to 0 on success, 1 if format is missing, 2 for invalid format, or 3 for invalid arguments.

sprintf2.summary: Produces C printf-style formatted output and stores the result in the variable strvar. Sets result to 0 on success, 1 if format is missing, 2 for invalid format, 3 for invalid argument, or 4 for invalid destination variable.

str2code.summary: Converts <string> to its ASCII code value(s) and stores the result in the integer variable <intvar>. Holds at most 4 ASCII codes; if the string is longer, only the last 4 bytes are copied.

str2int.summary: Converts <string> to its numeric value (decimal, or hexadecimal as 0x7b or $7b) and stores it in <intvar>. Sets result to 1 on success or 0 on failure; only the leading numeric portion is converted.

strcompare.summary: Performs a lexicographic comparison of two strings and stores the outcome in result: -1 if string1 < string2, 0 if equal, or 1 if string1 > string2.

strconcat.summary: Appends a copy of <string> to the end of the string variable <strvar>, modifying it in place with the combined result.

strcopy.summary: Extracts a substring from <string> starting at position <pos> (1-based) of length <len> and stores the result in the string variable <strvar>.

strinsert.summary: Inserts <string> into the string variable <strvar> at position <index> (1-based; position 1 is before the first character). Indices outside 1 to length+1 cause a syntax error.

strjoin.summary: Concatenates the system variables groupmatchstr1 through groupmatchstr9 into one string, inserting <separator> between elements, and stores it in <strvar>. <count> limits how many are joined (default 9).

strlen.summary: Counts the number of bytes in <string> and stores the count in the system variable result. An empty or null string yields 0.

strmatch.summary: Matches <target string> against a regular expression. Sets result to 0 for no match or the matched offset (1-origin) on a match; the matched text goes to matchstr and captured groups to groupmatchstr1 through groupmatchstr9.

strremove.summary: Deletes <len> characters from the string variable <strvar> starting at position <index> (1-based), modifying the variable in place.

strreplace.summary: Replaces the first regex match in <strvar> starting at <index> (1-origin) with <newstr> (deleting the match if <newstr> is empty). Sets result to 1 on success (matchstr holds the match), 0 if no match, or -1 for invalid regex.

strscan.summary: Searches for <substring> within <string> and stores the position of the first occurrence (1-based) in result, or 0 if not found.

strspecial.summary: Converts escape sequences such as backslash-n and backslash-t into their special characters. With one argument it converts <strvar> in place; with two it converts <strval> and stores the result in <strvar>.

strsplit.summary: Splits <strval> by the delimiter <separator> into the system variables groupmatchstr1 through groupmatchstr9. <count> (max 9) limits the number of pieces; result holds the actual number of divisions (10 when more than 9 with count omitted).

strtrim.summary: Removes all characters in the set <trimchars> from the beginning and end of the string variable <strvar>, storing the trimmed result back in <strvar>.

tolower.summary: Converts all alphabetic characters in <string> to lowercase and returns the result in the string variable <strvar>; non-alphabetic characters are unchanged.

toupper.summary: Converts all alphabetic characters in <string> to uppercase and returns the result in the string variable <strvar>; non-alphabetic characters are unchanged.

delpassword.summary: Deletes the password identified by <password name> from the password file <filename>. If <password name> is a blank string, all passwords in the file are deleted.

delpassword2.summary: Deletes the password identified by <password name> from the password file <filename> created by setpassword2 or getpassword2. If <password name> is blank, all passwords in the file are deleted.

getpassword.summary: Retrieves the encrypted password named <password name> from <filename>, decrypts it, and stores it in <password var>. Missing files are created; an unknown name prompts the user via a dialog and saves it. Sets result to 1 on success or 0 if the file cannot be written.

getpassword2.summary: Retrieves the password named <password name> from <filename>, decrypts it using <encrypt str>, and stores it in <password var>. Missing files are created; an unknown name prompts the user and saves it. Sets result to 1 on success or 0 if the file cannot be written.

ispassword.summary: Checks whether the password identified by <password name> is stored in the password file <filename>. Sets result to 1 if found, or 0 if not.

ispassword2.summary: Checks whether the password identified by <password name> exists in the password file <filename> created by setpassword2 or getpassword2. Sets result to 1 if found, or 0 if not.

passwordbox.summary: Opens a dialog prompting the user for a password, displaying the input as asterisks. The entered password is stored in the system variable inputstr.

setpassword.summary: Stores or updates the encrypted <password> identified by <password name> in the password file <filename> (created if it does not exist; multiple entries allowed). Sets result to 1 on success or 0 if the file cannot be written.

setpassword2.summary: Stores or updates <password> identified by <password name> in <filename>, encrypted with <encrypt str> using AES-256-CTR (file created if needed; multiple entries allowed). Sets result to 1 on success or 0 if the file cannot be written.
